name: Department Of Corrections
description_short: Department of Corrections is a federally-insured credit union based in Baton Rouge, LA. Member-owned and not-for-profit, it provides everyday banking, lending, and savings services to its membership.
description_long: Department Of Corrections is a state-chartered credit union serving members in , , established in 1957. As a not-for-profit financial cooperative, credit unions typically offer lower loan rates, higher savings rates, and fewer fees than traditional banks. The credit union operates 10 branch locations. Deposits are federally insured up to $250,000 by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA).
services: Personal loans (typically lower rates than banks)
pros: Not-for-profit — typically lower fees and better rates than banks
NCUA insured — deposits protected up to $250,000
cons: Membership requirements may apply (geographic, employer, or affiliation)
Fewer branches and ATMs than large banks
May have limited digital banking features
best_for: Lower fees and better loan rates than banks
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Department of Corrections (Baton Rouge, LA) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Department of Corrections — NCUA-insured credit union in Baton Rouge, LA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
